message:
feat(pager): Bayesian e-value eviction scoring (IMPL-19 / AAC-P5)

Introduces a safe, anytime-valid test-martingale / e-process for page cache
eviction, gated behind the new `evalue-eviction` cargo feature.

Math
----
For each tracked page P, maintain e_P in R+:
  on access:  e_P <- e_P * r_hit    (r_hit = 1.5)
  per tick:   e_P <- e_P * r_tick   (r_tick = 0.95)
Under the null "P is cold", (e_P(t)) is a non-negative supermartingale with
E[e_P(0)] <= 1. By Ville's inequality (Ville 1939),
    P( sup_t e_P(t) >= 1/alpha | null ) <= alpha
at any stopping time -- a "safe, anytime-valid" bound independent of the
eviction loop's stopping rule. Ranking by smallest e_P therefore evicts
pages whose cold-page null is hardest to reject; `ville_pvalue(page) = 1/e_P`
exposes the implied Type-I error level to callers that want to gate
evictions on statistical significance. The Bayesian-mixture property
(convex combinations of e-processes are e-processes) holds by linearity of
expectation -- relied on implicitly when tuning r_hit.

Implementation
--------------
* New module `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/evalue_eviction.rs` (~590 lines).
* `EValueEvictor` uses `DashMap<PageNumber, AtomicF64>`. AtomicF64 is a
  safe wrapper around `AtomicU64` via `f64::to_bits` / `f64::from_bits`
  (both are `safe fn` in stable Rust -- no `unsafe` needed, satisfying the
  workspace `unsafe_code = "forbid"` lint).
* `record_access` is a lock-free CAS loop; `tick` scans the map and is
  called automatically every `DEFAULT_TICK_INTERVAL = 1024` accesses, or
  manually via `tick` / `tick_n`.
* E-values clamped to [1e-30, 1e30] to prevent underflow/overflow while
  preserving ordering for any realistic workload.

Integration
-----------
Actual wiring (not just scaffolding) into `ShardedPageCache`:
* Adds `evalue_evictor` field (gated on feature).
* `record_eviction_access` / `record_eviction_admit` feed the evictor.
* `forget_eviction_page` / `clear_eviction_history` keep its state in sync.
* `evalue_record_access` auto-ticks every 1024 accesses.
* New public API: `evalue_choose_victim`, `evalue_ville_pvalue`,
  `evalue_evictor` accessor.
* Existing `evict_any` / S3-FIFO policy remain the default; callers
  explicitly opt in by consulting `evalue_choose_victim`. This preserves
  the existing eviction behaviour behind the feature flag so the flag is
  a no-op when off.

Tests (11, all passing)
-----------------------
* `record_access_creates_and_grows_entry` -- insertion + r_hit multiply.
* `tick_decays_unaccessed_pages_toward_zero` -- decay to floor.
* `hot_page_grows_as_r_hit_pow_t` -- balanced null boundary + pure growth.
* `mixture_property_accessed_half_the_time_stays_above_alpha` --
  Ville bound: 50% access rate => e_P > 20, p-value < 0.05 at t=100.
* `choose_victim_picks_minimum_e_value` -- argmin over candidates.
* `ville_pvalue_is_one_over_e` -- 1/e_P identity + untracked = 1.
* `forget_removes_tracking` -- eviction bookkeeping.
* `choose_victim_handles_untracked_candidates` -- null-boundary fallback.
* `tick_n_matches_repeated_tick` -- batched decay equivalence.
* `clamp_prevents_overflow_and_underflow` -- numerical bounds.
* `concurrent_record_access_preserves_growth` -- 8-thread CAS contention.

Verification
------------
* `cargo check -p fsqlite-pager --features evalue-eviction`: clean.
* `cargo check -p fsqlite-pager` (feature off): clean, zero cost.
* `cargo test -p fsqlite-pager --features evalue-eviction evalue`: 11/11.
* `cargo test -p fsqlite-pager --features evalue-eviction page_cache`:
  81/81 (no regressions from the integration).
* `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager --features evalue-eviction --lib --tests`:
  clean.
* `cargo fmt`: clean.

References: Shafer 2021 "Testing by betting"; Ramdas/Grunwald/Vovk/Shafer
2023 "Game-theoretic statistics and safe anytime-valid inference".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,6 +13,11 @@ native = ["fsqlite-types/native", "fsqlite-vfs/native", "fsqlite-wal/native"]
 wasm = ["fsqlite-types/wasm", "fsqlite-vfs/wasm", "fsqlite-wal/wasm"]
 linux-asupersync-uring = ["fsqlite-vfs/linux-asupersync-uring"]
 fault-injection = ["fsqlite-wal/fault-injection"]
+# Bayesian-mixture e-value eviction scoring (IMPL-19 / AAC-P5).
+# When enabled, ShardedPageCache records accesses into an EValueEvictor and
+# prefers the lowest-e page for eviction. Falls back to the existing policy
+# when disabled (compile-out).
+evalue-eviction = []
 
 [dependencies]
 foldhash = "0.1"
